xanesnet.models.e3ee_full.layers package¶

Public API for E3EEFull layer modules.

class xanesnet.models.e3ee_full.layers.AllAtomAtomAttention(atom_dim, e_dim, hidden_dim, latent_dim, att_cutoff, rbf_dim=16, max_z=100, z_emb_dim=32, n_heads=4)[source]¶

Bases: Module

Sparse energy-conditioned attention with one query per atom and one key/value per edge.

The attention scope (which atoms each query may attend to) is supplied externally as the att_src/att_dst edge list – typically a radius graph larger than the local encoder graph. RBF-encoded distances enter the keys/values, and values receive an additional cosine cutoff envelope. Softmax over each query’s edge set provides renormalization.

The optional absorber_mask lets the caller restrict queries to absorber atoms when use_absorber_mask is enabled in the parent model; other rows of the output are zeros.

Parameters:

  • atom_dim (int) – Dimension of invariant per-atom features.

  • e_dim (int) – Dimension of the energy RBF embedding.

  • hidden_dim (int) – Hidden dimension of all internal MLPs.

  • latent_dim (int) – Output (latent) dimension; must be divisible by n_heads.

  • att_cutoff (float) – Attention neighborhood radius in A.

  • rbf_dim (int) – Number of Gaussian RBF bases for distance encoding.

  • max_z (int) – Maximum atomic number supported by the element embedding.

  • z_emb_dim (int) – Embedding dimension for atomic numbers.

  • n_heads (int) – Number of attention heads.

__init__(atom_dim, e_dim, hidden_dim, latent_dim, att_cutoff, rbf_dim=16, max_z=100, z_emb_dim=32, n_heads=4)[source]¶

Initialize AllAtomAtomAttention.

forward(h, z, mask, e_feat, att_src, att_dst, att_dist, absorber_mask=None)[source]¶

Compute energy-conditioned attention over atoms and return latent.

Parameters:

  • h (Tensor) – Invariant atom features, shape (B, N, H).

  • z (Tensor) – Atomic numbers, shape (B, N).

  • mask (Tensor) – Valid-atom mask (encoder scope), shape (B, N).

  • e_feat (Tensor) – Energy RBF features, shape (nE, dE).

  • att_src (Tensor) – Flat source indices (queries) into B*N, shape (E_att,).

  • att_dst (Tensor) – Flat destination indices (keys/values) into B*N, shape (E_att,).

  • att_dist (Tensor) – Pair distances in A, shape (E_att,).

  • absorber_mask (Tensor | None) – Optional (B, N) bool mask. When given, queries are restricted to atoms with True; other rows are returned as zeros.

Return type:

Tensor

Returns:

Latent tensor of shape (B, N, nE, latent_dim).

class xanesnet.models.e3ee_full.layers.AllAtomAtomConvolution(atom_dim, e_dim, hidden_dim, latent_dim, att_cutoff, rbf_dim=16, max_z=100, z_emb_dim=32, use_gate=True)[source]¶

Bases: Module

Invariant SchNet/PaiNN-style continuous-filter convolution branch.

For every active receiver atom, computes a SchNet-style continuous-filter message from each neighbor, aggregates by sum, then multiplies with an energy-dependent gate to produce per-(atom, energy) latent vectors.

Parameters:

  • atom_dim (int) – Dimension of invariant per-atom features.

  • e_dim (int) – Dimension of the energy RBF embedding.

  • hidden_dim (int) – Hidden dimension of all internal MLPs.

  • latent_dim (int) – Output (latent) dimension.

  • att_cutoff (float) – Attention neighborhood radius in A.

  • rbf_dim (int) – Number of Gaussian RBF bases for distance encoding.

  • max_z (int) – Maximum atomic number supported by the element embedding.

  • z_emb_dim (int) – Embedding dimension for atomic numbers.

  • use_gate (bool) – If True, apply a PaiNN-style scalar gate per edge.

__init__(atom_dim, e_dim, hidden_dim, latent_dim, att_cutoff, rbf_dim=16, max_z=100, z_emb_dim=32, use_gate=True)[source]¶

Initialize AllAtomAtomConvolution.

forward(h, z, mask, e_feat, att_src, att_dst, att_dist, absorber_mask=None)[source]¶

Compute invariant convolution branch latent.

Parameters:

  • h (Tensor) – Invariant atom features, shape (B, N, H).

  • z (Tensor) – Atomic numbers, shape (B, N).

  • mask (Tensor) – Valid-atom mask, shape (B, N).

  • e_feat (Tensor) – Energy RBF features, shape (nE, dE).

  • att_src (Tensor) – Flat source indices (receivers) into B*N, shape (E_att,).

  • att_dst (Tensor) – Flat destination indices (senders) into B*N, shape (E_att,).

  • att_dist (Tensor) – Pair distances in A, shape (E_att,).

  • absorber_mask (Tensor | None) – Optional (B, N) bool mask; if given, restrict receivers to absorber sites.

Return type:

Tensor

Returns:

Latent tensor of shape (B, N, nE, latent_dim).

class xanesnet.models.e3ee_full.layers.AllAtomEnergyBranch(atom_dim, e_dim, hidden_dim, out_dim)[source]¶

Bases: Module

Energy-dependent branch applied to every atom’s invariant features.

Broadcasts the energy embedding over all atoms and passes the concatenation through an MLP to produce per-(atom, energy) latent vectors.

Parameters:

  • atom_dim (int) – Dimension of invariant per-atom features.

  • e_dim (int) – Dimension of the energy RBF embedding.

  • hidden_dim (int) – Hidden dimension of the internal MLP.

  • out_dim (int) – Output (latent) dimension.

__init__(atom_dim, e_dim, hidden_dim, out_dim)[source]¶

Initialize AllAtomEnergyBranch.

forward(h_all, e_feat)[source]¶

Compute per-(atom, energy) latent vectors.

Parameters:

  • h_all (Tensor) – Invariant features for every atom, shape (B, N, H).

  • e_feat (Tensor) – Energy RBF features, shape (nE, dE).

Return type:

Tensor

Returns:

Latent tensor of shape (B, N, nE, out_dim).

class xanesnet.models.e3ee_full.layers.AllAtomEquivariantAtomAttention(atom_dim, irreps_node, e_dim, hidden_dim, latent_dim, att_cutoff, attention_lmax, attention_irreps, rbf_dim=16, max_z=100, z_emb_dim=32, n_heads=4)[source]¶

Bases: Module

All-atom equivariant counterpart of AllAtomAtomAttention.

Each atom (or just the absorber atoms when use_absorber_mask is set) queries its attention-graph neighbors. The value carried along each edge is an E(3)-equivariant feature built from spherical harmonics of the src->dst unit vector mixed with the dst atom’s full equivariant features via a FullyConnectedTensorProduct, then modulated per-energy with EnergyIrrepModulation. Aggregated equivariant features are converted to invariants and projected to latent_dim.

Parameters:

  • atom_dim (int) – Dimension of invariant per-atom features (used for scoring).

  • irreps_node (Irreps) – Irreps of the full equivariant atom features (TP input).

  • e_dim (int) – Dimension of the energy RBF embedding.

  • hidden_dim (int) – Hidden dimension of all internal MLPs.

  • latent_dim (int) – Output (latent) dimension; must be divisible by n_heads.

  • att_cutoff (float) – Attention neighborhood radius in A.

  • attention_lmax (int) – Maximum spherical-harmonics order for bond directions.

  • attention_irreps (str) – Target irreps of the equivariant values (e.g. "32x0e+16x1o").

  • rbf_dim (int) – Number of Gaussian RBF bases for distance encoding.

  • max_z (int) – Maximum atomic number supported by the element embedding.

  • z_emb_dim (int) – Embedding dimension for atomic numbers.

  • n_heads (int) – Number of attention heads used for invariant scoring.

__init__(atom_dim, irreps_node, e_dim, hidden_dim, latent_dim, att_cutoff, attention_lmax, attention_irreps, rbf_dim=16, max_z=100, z_emb_dim=32, n_heads=4)[source]¶

Initialize AllAtomEquivariantAtomAttention.

forward(h, h_full, z, mask, e_feat, att_src, att_dst, att_dist, att_vec, absorber_mask=None)[source]¶

Compute equivariant energy-conditioned attention over atoms and return latent.

Parameters:

  • h (Tensor) – Invariant atom features (used for scoring), shape (B, N, H).

  • h_full (Tensor) – Full equivariant atom features (TP input for values), shape (B, N, irreps_node.dim).

  • z (Tensor) – Atomic numbers, shape (B, N).

  • mask (Tensor) – Valid-atom mask, shape (B, N).

  • e_feat (Tensor) – Energy RBF features, shape (nE, dE).

  • att_src (Tensor) – Flat source indices (queries) into B*N, shape (E_att,).

  • att_dst (Tensor) – Flat destination indices (keys/values) into B*N, shape (E_att,).

  • att_dist (Tensor) – Pair distances in A, shape (E_att,).

  • att_vec (Tensor) – Pair displacement vectors (src->dst) in A, shape (E_att, 3).

  • absorber_mask (Tensor | None) – Optional (B, N) bool mask. When given, queries are restricted to atoms with True; other rows are returned as zeros.

Return type:

Tensor

Returns:

Latent tensor of shape (B, N, nE, latent_dim).

class xanesnet.models.e3ee_full.layers.AllAtomEquivariantAtomConvolution(atom_dim, irreps_node, e_dim, hidden_dim, latent_dim, att_cutoff, attention_lmax, attention_irreps, rbf_dim=16, max_z=100, z_emb_dim=32, use_gate=True)[source]¶

Bases: Module

NequIP/MACE-style equivariant convolution branch.

For every active receiver atom (or absorber when use_absorber_mask is enabled):

  • sh_{a<-j} = Y(u_{a<-j}) – spherical harmonics of bond direction.

  • v_{a<-j} = TP(h_full[j], sh_{a<-j}; W(z_j, RBF, is_self)) – NequIP-style equivariant message.

  • v_{a<-j} *= cos_envelope(dist) – smooth radial cutoff.

  • (Optional) v_{a<-j} *= sigmoid(MLP(h_src, h_dst, RBF)) – PaiNN-style scalar gate.

  • v_a = sum_j v_{a<-j} – sum aggregation.

  • v_{a,e} = EnergyIrrepModulation(v_a, e_feat) – per-receiver energy mod.

  • out = MLP(invariants(v_{a,e}))

Self-edges (dist = 0) are gated to Y_l = delta_{l,0} so they only contribute through the l=0 channel. Memory: per-edge tensors are (E, D_irrep) – the energy axis only appears after aggregation.

Parameters:

  • atom_dim (int) – Dimension of invariant per-atom features.

  • irreps_node (Irreps) – Irreps of the full equivariant atom features (TP input).

  • e_dim (int) – Dimension of the energy RBF embedding.

  • hidden_dim (int) – Hidden dimension of all internal MLPs.

  • latent_dim (int) – Output (latent) dimension.

  • att_cutoff (float) – Attention neighborhood radius in A.

  • attention_lmax (int) – Maximum spherical-harmonics order for bond directions.

  • attention_irreps (str) – Target irreps of the equivariant messages (e.g. "32x0e+16x1o").

  • rbf_dim (int) – Number of Gaussian RBF bases for distance encoding.

  • max_z (int) – Maximum atomic number supported by the element embedding.

  • z_emb_dim (int) – Embedding dimension for atomic numbers.

  • use_gate (bool) – If True, apply a PaiNN-style scalar gate per edge.

__init__(atom_dim, irreps_node, e_dim, hidden_dim, latent_dim, att_cutoff, attention_lmax, attention_irreps, rbf_dim=16, max_z=100, z_emb_dim=32, use_gate=True)[source]¶

Initialize AllAtomEquivariantAtomConvolution.

Compute equivariant convolution branch latent.

Parameters:

  • h (Tensor) – Invariant atom features, shape (B, N, H).

  • h_full (Tensor) – Full equivariant atom features, shape (B, N, irreps_node.dim).

  • z (Tensor) – Atomic numbers, shape (B, N).

  • mask (Tensor) – Valid-atom mask, shape (B, N).

  • e_feat (Tensor) – Energy RBF features, shape (nE, dE).

  • att_src (Tensor) – Flat source indices (receivers) into B*N, shape (E_att,).

  • att_dst (Tensor) – Flat destination indices (senders) into B*N, shape (E_att,).

  • att_dist (Tensor) – Pair distances in A, shape (E_att,).

  • att_vec (Tensor) – Pair displacement vectors in A, shape (E_att, 3).

  • absorber_mask (Tensor | None) – Optional (B, N) bool mask; if given, restrict receivers to absorber sites.

Return type:

class xanesnet.models.e3ee_full.layers.AllAtomEquivariantHead(irreps_node, e_dim, hidden_dim, out_dim)[source]¶

Bases: Module

Late equivariant head applied to every atom.

Applies energy-conditioned irrep-wise modulation to each atom’s equivariant feature, converts to invariants, then projects to out_dim.

Parameters:

  • irreps_node (Irreps) – Irreps of the input equivariant features.

  • e_dim (int) – Dimension of the energy RBF embedding.

  • hidden_dim (int) – Hidden dimension of all internal MLPs.

  • out_dim (int) – Output (latent) dimension.

  • e_feat (Tensor) – Energy RBF features, shape (nE, e_dim).

Tensor

Latent tensor of shape (B, N, nE, out_dim).

class xanesnet.models.e3ee_full.layers.AllAtomPathAggregator(atom_dim, rbf_dim, geom_hidden_dim, scatter_dim, out_dim, cutoff)[source]¶

Bases: Module

Per-site 3-body path aggregator.

Consumes precomputed flat triplet scalars (r0j, r0k, rjk, cos(angle)) together with path_center – the flat atom index of the site (into the padded B * N layout) that each path belongs to. Paths are scatter-aggregated into the per-atom layout and projected.

Parameters:

  • atom_dim (int) – Dimension of invariant per-atom features.

  • rbf_dim (int) – Number of Gaussian RBF bases for distance encoding.

  • geom_hidden_dim (int) – Hidden dimension of the geometry MLP.

  • scatter_dim (int) – Intermediate scatter feature dimension.

  • out_dim (int) – Output (latent) dimension.

  • cutoff (float) – Radial cutoff in A used for the cosine envelope weights.

__init__(atom_dim, rbf_dim, geom_hidden_dim, scatter_dim, out_dim, cutoff)[source]¶

Initialize AllAtomPathAggregator.

forward(h_flat, z_flat, pair_elem_energy, e_feat, path_center, path_j, path_k, path_r0j, path_r0k, path_rjk, path_cosangle, bsz, n_atoms)[source]¶

Scatter 3-body path features into per-(atom, energy) latent vectors.

Parameters:

  • h_flat (Tensor) – Invariant atom features (flat), shape (B*N, atom_dim).

  • z_flat (Tensor) – Atomic numbers (flat), shape (B*N,).

  • pair_elem_energy (PairElementEnergyScattering) – Pre-constructed element-pair energy scattering module.

  • e_feat (Tensor) – Energy RBF features, shape (nE, e_dim).

  • path_center (Tensor) – Flat absorber-site index per path into B*N, shape (P,).

  • path_j (Tensor) – Flat atom index for leg j into B*N, shape (P,).

  • path_k (Tensor) – Flat atom index for leg k into B*N, shape (P,).

  • path_r0j (Tensor) – Absorber-to-j distance in A, shape (P,).

  • path_r0k (Tensor) – Absorber-to-k distance in A, shape (P,).

  • path_rjk (Tensor) – j-to-k distance in A, shape (P,).

  • path_cosangle (Tensor) – Cosine of the j-absorber-k angle, shape (P,).

  • bsz (int) – Batch size.

  • n_atoms (int) – Padded atoms per sample (N_max).

Return type:

Tensor

Returns:

Latent tensor of shape (B, N, nE, out_dim).

class xanesnet.models.e3ee_full.layers.CosineCutoff(cutoff)[source]¶

Bases: Module

Smooth cosine cutoff envelope.

Smoothly decays from 1 at r = 0 to 0 at r = cutoff.

Parameters:

cutoff (float) – Cutoff radius in A beyond which the envelope is zero.

__init__(cutoff)[source]¶

Initialize CosineCutoff.

forward(r)[source]¶

Evaluate cosine envelope.

Parameters:

r (Tensor) – Interatomic distances of arbitrary shape (...) in A.

Return type:

Tensor

Returns:

Envelope values in [0, 1] with the same shape as r.

class xanesnet.models.e3ee_full.layers.EnergyIrrepModulation(irreps, e_dim, hidden_dim)[source]¶

Bases: Module

Energy-conditioned scalar modulation of each irrep copy, preserving equivariance.

A scalar gate per irrep copy is predicted from the energy embedding and applied multiplicatively to all dim-dimensional components of that copy. Because a scalar multiplier commutes with rotations, equivariance is preserved.

Input x has shape (M, D); output broadcasts over energies to (M, nE, D).

Parameters:

  • irreps (Irreps) – The irreps specification of the equivariant features.

  • e_dim (int) – Dimension of the energy RBF embedding.

  • hidden_dim (int) – Hidden dimension of the internal MLP.

__init__(irreps, e_dim, hidden_dim)[source]¶

Initialize EnergyIrrepModulation.

forward(x, e_feat)[source]¶

Apply per-copy scalar energy modulation.

Parameters:

  • x (Tensor) – Equivariant features of shape (M, D) where M = B * N.

  • e_feat (Tensor) – Energy RBF features, shape (nE, e_dim).

Return type:

Tensor

Returns:

Modulated equivariant features of shape (M, nE, D).

class xanesnet.models.e3ee_full.layers.EquivariantAtomEncoder(max_z, cutoff, num_interactions, rbf_dim, lmax, node_attr_dim, hidden_dim, irreps_node, irreps_message, residual_scale_init)[source]¶

Bases: Module

Absorber-agnostic equivariant atom encoder with spherical-harmonics message passing.

Unlike the single-absorber E3EE encoder, this variant is absorber-agnostic: the scalar node input contains only the element embedding – no absorber flag is used. The encoder produces a per-atom equivariant feature for every atom in the padded layout, suitable for dense (all-absorber) prediction.

Parameters:

  • max_z (int) – Maximum atomic number supported by the element embedding.

  • cutoff (float) – Radial cutoff for the message-passing graph in A.

  • num_interactions (int) – Number of equivariant interaction blocks.

  • rbf_dim (int) – Number of Gaussian RBF bases for distance encoding.

  • lmax (int) – Maximum spherical-harmonics order.

  • node_attr_dim (int) – Dimension of the initial element embedding.

  • hidden_dim (int) – Hidden dimension used in the radial MLP.

  • irreps_node (str) – Target irreps of the node features (e.g. "64x0e+32x1o").

  • irreps_message (str) – Irreps of the intermediate message tensors.

  • residual_scale_init (float) – Initial scale of the learnable residual.

__init__(max_z, cutoff, num_interactions, rbf_dim, lmax, node_attr_dim, hidden_dim, irreps_node, irreps_message, residual_scale_init)[source]¶

Initialize EquivariantAtomEncoder.

forward(z, mask, edge_src, edge_dst, edge_weight, edge_vec)[source]¶

Encode all atoms into equivariant features via multi-layer message passing.

Parameters:

  • z (Tensor) – Atomic numbers (int64), shape (B, N).

  • mask (Tensor) – Valid-atom mask, shape (B, N).

  • edge_src (Tensor) – Source flat indices into B*N, shape (E,).

  • edge_dst (Tensor) – Destination flat indices into B*N, shape (E,).

  • edge_weight (Tensor) – Edge lengths in A (PBC-correct), shape (E,).

  • edge_vec (Tensor) – Edge displacement vectors in A (PBC-correct), shape (E, 3).

Return type:

Tensor

Returns:

Equivariant atom features of shape (B, N, irreps_dim).

class xanesnet.models.e3ee_full.layers.EquivariantInteractionBlock(irreps_node, irreps_sh, irreps_message, rbf_dim, radial_hidden_dim, cutoff, residual_scale_init=0.1)[source]¶

Bases: Module

Single equivariant message-passing interaction block using e3nn tensor products with gating.

Parameters:

  • irreps_node (str) – Irreps of the per-atom node features (input and output).

  • irreps_sh (str) – Irreps of the spherical harmonics on each edge.

  • irreps_message (str) – Intermediate irreps of the message before gating.

  • rbf_dim (int) – Number of Gaussian RBF features encoding edge lengths.

  • radial_hidden_dim (int) – Hidden dimension of the radial weight MLP.

  • cutoff (float) – Smooth cutoff radius in A; edges beyond this have zero weight.

  • residual_scale_init (float) – Initial value for the learnable residual scale.

__init__(irreps_node, irreps_sh, irreps_message, rbf_dim, radial_hidden_dim, cutoff, residual_scale_init=0.1)[source]¶

Initialize EquivariantInteractionBlock.

Apply one equivariant message-passing step.

Parameters:

  • x (Tensor) – Node features of shape (B*N, irreps_node.dim).

  • edge_src (Tensor) – Source flat indices into B*N, shape (E,).

  • edge_dst (Tensor) – Destination flat indices into B*N, shape (E,).

  • edge_sh (Tensor) – Spherical harmonics on each edge, shape (E, irreps_sh.dim).

  • edge_rbf (Tensor) – RBF-encoded edge lengths, shape (E, rbf_dim).

  • edge_len (Tensor) – Raw edge lengths in A, shape (E,).

Return type:

Tensor

Returns:

Updated node features of shape (B*N, irreps_node.dim).

Gaussian radial basis function expansion.

Parameters:

  • stop (float) – Right edge of the RBF center grid.

  • n_rbf (int) – Number of radial basis functions.

  • gamma (float | None) – Width parameter 1 / sigma^2. Defaults to 1 / delta^2 where delta is the uniform spacing between centers.

class xanesnet.models.e3ee_full.layers.GatedBranchFusion(branch_dims, fused_dim, cond_dim, hidden_dim, use_softmax=True)[source]¶

Bases: Module

Energy-conditioned gated fusion of active branch latents.

Each active branch is projected to fused_dim and combined with a learned soft gate predicted from the projected branch values, their magnitudes, and a conditioning feature such as the energy embedding.

Parameters:

  • branch_dims (list[int]) – Feature dimensions of the active branch tensors.

  • fused_dim (int) – Common latent dimension used for gated summation.

  • cond_dim (int) – Dimension of the conditioning features.

  • hidden_dim (int) – Hidden dimension of the gate and output MLPs.

  • use_softmax (bool) – If True, normalize gates with softmax. Otherwise, use sigmoid gates normalized to sum to one.

__init__(branch_dims, fused_dim, cond_dim, hidden_dim, use_softmax=True)[source]¶

Initialize GatedBranchFusion.

reset_gate_logits()[source]¶

Initialize the final gate layer to produce uniform gates.

Return type:

None

forward(branches, cond_feat, return_gates=False)[source]¶

Fuse active branch tensors.

Parameters:

  • branches (list[Tensor]) – Active branch tensors with matching leading dimensions. Each tensor has shape (..., branch_dim_i).

  • cond_feat (Tensor) – Conditioning features of shape (..., cond_dim) or any trailing-leading shape broadcastable to the branches, such as (nE, cond_dim) for branch tensors shaped (B, N, nE, D).

  • return_gates (bool) – If True, return the normalized gate weights along with the fused tensor.

Return type:

Tensor | tuple[Tensor, Tensor]

Returns:

Fused tensor of shape (..., fused_dim). If return_gates is True, returns (fused, gates) where gates has shape (..., n_branches).

class xanesnet.models.e3ee_full.layers.IrrepNorm(irreps, eps=1e-08, affine=True)[source]¶

Bases: Module

Irrep-respecting normalization.

For l = 0 blocks the multiplicity channels are mean/variance normalized. For l > 0 blocks each irrep copy is RMS-normalized across its irrep dimension.

Parameters:

  • irreps (Irreps) – The irreps specification that describes the feature layout.

  • eps (float) – Small constant added to denominators for numerical stability.

  • affine (bool) – If True, learn per-channel scale and bias parameters.

__init__(irreps, eps=1e-08, affine=True)[source]¶

Initialize IrrepNorm.

forward(x)[source]¶

Apply irrep-respecting normalization.

Parameters:

x (Tensor) – Input features of shape (..., irreps.dim).

Return type:

Tensor

Returns:

Normalized features with the same shape as x.

Parameters:

  • in_dim (int) – Size of the input features.

  • hidden_dim (int) – Size of each hidden layer.

  • out_dim (int) – Size of the output features.

  • n_layers (int) – Total number of linear layers (including the output layer).

  • dropout (float) – Dropout probability applied after each hidden activation. Set to 0.0 to disable.

  • layer_norm (bool) – If True, apply LayerNorm after each hidden linear layer.

Tensor

Submodules¶

xanesnet.models.e3ee_full.layers.atom_encoder module¶

Absorber-agnostic equivariant atom encoder with spherical-harmonics message passing.

class xanesnet.models.e3ee_full.layers.atom_encoder.EquivariantAtomEncoder(max_z, cutoff, num_interactions, rbf_dim, lmax, node_attr_dim, hidden_dim, irreps_node, irreps_message, residual_scale_init)[source]¶

Bases: Module

Absorber-agnostic equivariant atom encoder with spherical-harmonics message passing.

Unlike the single-absorber E3EE encoder, this variant is absorber-agnostic: the scalar node input contains only the element embedding – no absorber flag is used. The encoder produces a per-atom equivariant feature for every atom in the padded layout, suitable for dense (all-absorber) prediction.

Parameters:

  • max_z (int) – Maximum atomic number supported by the element embedding.

  • cutoff (float) – Radial cutoff for the message-passing graph in A.

  • num_interactions (int) – Number of equivariant interaction blocks.

  • rbf_dim (int) – Number of Gaussian RBF bases for distance encoding.

  • lmax (int) – Maximum spherical-harmonics order.

  • node_attr_dim (int) – Dimension of the initial element embedding.

  • hidden_dim (int) – Hidden dimension used in the radial MLP.

  • irreps_node (str) – Target irreps of the node features (e.g. "64x0e+32x1o").

  • irreps_message (str) – Irreps of the intermediate message tensors.

  • residual_scale_init (float) – Initial scale of the learnable residual.

__init__(max_z, cutoff, num_interactions, rbf_dim, lmax, node_attr_dim, hidden_dim, irreps_node, irreps_message, residual_scale_init)[source]¶

Initialize EquivariantAtomEncoder.

forward(z, mask, edge_src, edge_dst, edge_weight, edge_vec)[source]¶

Encode all atoms into equivariant features via multi-layer message passing.

Parameters:

  • z (Tensor) – Atomic numbers (int64), shape (B, N).

  • mask (Tensor) – Valid-atom mask, shape (B, N).

  • edge_src (Tensor) – Source flat indices into B*N, shape (E,).

  • edge_dst (Tensor) – Destination flat indices into B*N, shape (E,).

  • edge_weight (Tensor) – Edge lengths in A (PBC-correct), shape (E,).

  • edge_vec (Tensor) – Edge displacement vectors in A (PBC-correct), shape (E, 3).

Return type:

Tensor

Returns:

Equivariant atom features of shape (B, N, irreps_dim).

class xanesnet.models.e3ee_full.layers.basic.CosineCutoff(cutoff)[source]¶

Smoothly decays from 1 at r = 0 to 0 at r = cutoff.

xanesnet.models.e3ee_full.layers.branch_absorber module¶

Dense invariant energy-dependent branch applied to every atom in E3EEFull.

class xanesnet.models.e3ee_full.layers.branch_absorber.AllAtomEnergyBranch(atom_dim, e_dim, hidden_dim, out_dim)[source]¶

Bases: Module

Energy-dependent branch applied to every atom’s invariant features.

Broadcasts the energy embedding over all atoms and passes the concatenation through an MLP to produce per-(atom, energy) latent vectors.

Parameters:

  • atom_dim (int) – Dimension of invariant per-atom features.

  • e_dim (int) – Dimension of the energy RBF embedding.

  • hidden_dim (int) – Hidden dimension of the internal MLP.

  • out_dim (int) – Output (latent) dimension.

__init__(atom_dim, e_dim, hidden_dim, out_dim)[source]¶

Initialize AllAtomEnergyBranch.

forward(h_all, e_feat)[source]¶

Compute per-(atom, energy) latent vectors.

Parameters:

  • h_all (Tensor) – Invariant features for every atom, shape (B, N, H).

  • e_feat (Tensor) – Energy RBF features, shape (nE, dE).

Return type:

Tensor

Returns:

Latent tensor of shape (B, N, nE, out_dim).

xanesnet.models.e3ee_full.layers.branch_attention module¶

Energy-conditioned sparse atom-attention branch for E3EEFull.

class xanesnet.models.e3ee_full.layers.branch_attention.AllAtomAtomAttention(atom_dim, e_dim, hidden_dim, latent_dim, att_cutoff, rbf_dim=16, max_z=100, z_emb_dim=32, n_heads=4)[source]¶

Bases: Module

Sparse energy-conditioned attention with one query per atom and one key/value per edge.

The attention scope (which atoms each query may attend to) is supplied externally as the att_src/att_dst edge list – typically a radius graph larger than the local encoder graph. RBF-encoded distances enter the keys/values, and values receive an additional cosine cutoff envelope. Softmax over each query’s edge set provides renormalization.

The optional absorber_mask lets the caller restrict queries to absorber atoms when use_absorber_mask is enabled in the parent model; other rows of the output are zeros.

Parameters:

  • atom_dim (int) – Dimension of invariant per-atom features.

  • e_dim (int) – Dimension of the energy RBF embedding.

  • hidden_dim (int) – Hidden dimension of all internal MLPs.

  • latent_dim (int) – Output (latent) dimension; must be divisible by n_heads.

  • att_cutoff (float) – Attention neighborhood radius in A.

  • rbf_dim (int) – Number of Gaussian RBF bases for distance encoding.

  • max_z (int) – Maximum atomic number supported by the element embedding.

  • z_emb_dim (int) – Embedding dimension for atomic numbers.

  • n_heads (int) – Number of attention heads.

__init__(atom_dim, e_dim, hidden_dim, latent_dim, att_cutoff, rbf_dim=16, max_z=100, z_emb_dim=32, n_heads=4)[source]¶

Initialize AllAtomAtomAttention.

forward(h, z, mask, e_feat, att_src, att_dst, att_dist, absorber_mask=None)[source]¶

Compute energy-conditioned attention over atoms and return latent.

Parameters:

  • h (Tensor) – Invariant atom features, shape (B, N, H).

  • z (Tensor) – Atomic numbers, shape (B, N).

  • mask (Tensor) – Valid-atom mask (encoder scope), shape (B, N).

  • e_feat (Tensor) – Energy RBF features, shape (nE, dE).

  • att_src (Tensor) – Flat source indices (queries) into B*N, shape (E_att,).

  • att_dst (Tensor) – Flat destination indices (keys/values) into B*N, shape (E_att,).

  • att_dist (Tensor) – Pair distances in A, shape (E_att,).

  • absorber_mask (Tensor | None) – Optional (B, N) bool mask. When given, queries are restricted to atoms with True; other rows are returned as zeros.

Return type:

Tensor

Returns:

Latent tensor of shape (B, N, nE, latent_dim).

xanesnet.models.e3ee_full.layers.branch_convolution module¶

Energy-conditioned invariant and equivariant convolution branches for E3EEFull.

class xanesnet.models.e3ee_full.layers.branch_convolution.AllAtomAtomConvolution(atom_dim, e_dim, hidden_dim, latent_dim, att_cutoff, rbf_dim=16, max_z=100, z_emb_dim=32, use_gate=True)[source]¶

Bases: Module

Invariant SchNet/PaiNN-style continuous-filter convolution branch.

For every active receiver atom, computes a SchNet-style continuous-filter message from each neighbor, aggregates by sum, then multiplies with an energy-dependent gate to produce per-(atom, energy) latent vectors.

Parameters:

  • atom_dim (int) – Dimension of invariant per-atom features.

  • e_dim (int) – Dimension of the energy RBF embedding.

  • hidden_dim (int) – Hidden dimension of all internal MLPs.

  • latent_dim (int) – Output (latent) dimension.

  • att_cutoff (float) – Attention neighborhood radius in A.

  • rbf_dim (int) – Number of Gaussian RBF bases for distance encoding.

  • max_z (int) – Maximum atomic number supported by the element embedding.

  • z_emb_dim (int) – Embedding dimension for atomic numbers.

  • use_gate (bool) – If True, apply a PaiNN-style scalar gate per edge.

__init__(atom_dim, e_dim, hidden_dim, latent_dim, att_cutoff, rbf_dim=16, max_z=100, z_emb_dim=32, use_gate=True)[source]¶

Initialize AllAtomAtomConvolution.

forward(h, z, mask, e_feat, att_src, att_dst, att_dist, absorber_mask=None)[source]¶

Compute invariant convolution branch latent.

Parameters:

  • h (Tensor) – Invariant atom features, shape (B, N, H).

  • z (Tensor) – Atomic numbers, shape (B, N).

  • mask (Tensor) – Valid-atom mask, shape (B, N).

  • e_feat (Tensor) – Energy RBF features, shape (nE, dE).

  • att_src (Tensor) – Flat source indices (receivers) into B*N, shape (E_att,).

  • att_dst (Tensor) – Flat destination indices (senders) into B*N, shape (E_att,).

  • att_dist (Tensor) – Pair distances in A, shape (E_att,).

  • absorber_mask (Tensor | None) – Optional (B, N) bool mask; if given, restrict receivers to absorber sites.

Return type:

Tensor

Returns:

Latent tensor of shape (B, N, nE, latent_dim).

class xanesnet.models.e3ee_full.layers.branch_convolution.AllAtomEquivariantAtomConvolution(atom_dim, irreps_node, e_dim, hidden_dim, latent_dim, att_cutoff, attention_lmax, attention_irreps, rbf_dim=16, max_z=100, z_emb_dim=32, use_gate=True)[source]¶

Bases: Module

NequIP/MACE-style equivariant convolution branch.

For every active receiver atom (or absorber when use_absorber_mask is enabled):

  • sh_{a<-j} = Y(u_{a<-j}) – spherical harmonics of bond direction.

  • v_{a<-j} = TP(h_full[j], sh_{a<-j}; W(z_j, RBF, is_self)) – NequIP-style equivariant message.

  • v_{a<-j} *= cos_envelope(dist) – smooth radial cutoff.

  • (Optional) v_{a<-j} *= sigmoid(MLP(h_src, h_dst, RBF)) – PaiNN-style scalar gate.

  • v_a = sum_j v_{a<-j} – sum aggregation.

  • v_{a,e} = EnergyIrrepModulation(v_a, e_feat) – per-receiver energy mod.

  • out = MLP(invariants(v_{a,e}))

Self-edges (dist = 0) are gated to Y_l = delta_{l,0} so they only contribute through the l=0 channel. Memory: per-edge tensors are (E, D_irrep) – the energy axis only appears after aggregation.

Parameters:

  • atom_dim (int) – Dimension of invariant per-atom features.

  • irreps_node (Irreps) – Irreps of the full equivariant atom features (TP input).

  • e_dim (int) – Dimension of the energy RBF embedding.

  • hidden_dim (int) – Hidden dimension of all internal MLPs.

  • latent_dim (int) – Output (latent) dimension.

  • att_cutoff (float) – Attention neighborhood radius in A.

  • attention_lmax (int) – Maximum spherical-harmonics order for bond directions.

  • attention_irreps (str) – Target irreps of the equivariant messages (e.g. "32x0e+16x1o").

  • rbf_dim (int) – Number of Gaussian RBF bases for distance encoding.

  • max_z (int) – Maximum atomic number supported by the element embedding.

  • z_emb_dim (int) – Embedding dimension for atomic numbers.

  • use_gate (bool) – If True, apply a PaiNN-style scalar gate per edge.

__init__(atom_dim, irreps_node, e_dim, hidden_dim, latent_dim, att_cutoff, attention_lmax, attention_irreps, rbf_dim=16, max_z=100, z_emb_dim=32, use_gate=True)[source]¶

Initialize AllAtomEquivariantAtomConvolution.

forward(h, h_full, z, mask, e_feat, att_src, att_dst, att_dist, att_vec, absorber_mask=None)[source]¶

Compute equivariant convolution branch latent.

Parameters:

  • h (Tensor) – Invariant atom features, shape (B, N, H).

  • h_full (Tensor) – Full equivariant atom features, shape (B, N, irreps_node.dim).

  • z (Tensor) – Atomic numbers, shape (B, N).

  • mask (Tensor) – Valid-atom mask, shape (B, N).

  • e_feat (Tensor) – Energy RBF features, shape (nE, dE).

  • att_src (Tensor) – Flat source indices (receivers) into B*N, shape (E_att,).

  • att_dst (Tensor) – Flat destination indices (senders) into B*N, shape (E_att,).

  • att_dist (Tensor) – Pair distances in A, shape (E_att,).

  • att_vec (Tensor) – Pair displacement vectors in A, shape (E_att, 3).

  • absorber_mask (Tensor | None) – Optional (B, N) bool mask; if given, restrict receivers to absorber sites.

Return type:

Tensor

Returns:

Latent tensor of shape (B, N, nE, latent_dim).

xanesnet.models.e3ee_full.layers.branch_eq_attention module¶

Energy-conditioned equivariant atom-attention branch for E3EEFull.

class xanesnet.models.e3ee_full.layers.branch_eq_attention.AllAtomEquivariantAtomAttention(atom_dim, irreps_node, e_dim, hidden_dim, latent_dim, att_cutoff, attention_lmax, attention_irreps, rbf_dim=16, max_z=100, z_emb_dim=32, n_heads=4)[source]¶

Bases: Module

All-atom equivariant counterpart of AllAtomAtomAttention.

Each atom (or just the absorber atoms when use_absorber_mask is set) queries its attention-graph neighbors. The value carried along each edge is an E(3)-equivariant feature built from spherical harmonics of the src->dst unit vector mixed with the dst atom’s full equivariant features via a FullyConnectedTensorProduct, then modulated per-energy with EnergyIrrepModulation. Aggregated equivariant features are converted to invariants and projected to latent_dim.

Parameters:

  • atom_dim (int) – Dimension of invariant per-atom features (used for scoring).

  • irreps_node (Irreps) – Irreps of the full equivariant atom features (TP input).

  • e_dim (int) – Dimension of the energy RBF embedding.

  • hidden_dim (int) – Hidden dimension of all internal MLPs.

  • latent_dim (int) – Output (latent) dimension; must be divisible by n_heads.

  • att_cutoff (float) – Attention neighborhood radius in A.

  • attention_lmax (int) – Maximum spherical-harmonics order for bond directions.

  • attention_irreps (str) – Target irreps of the equivariant values (e.g. "32x0e+16x1o").

  • rbf_dim (int) – Number of Gaussian RBF bases for distance encoding.

  • max_z (int) – Maximum atomic number supported by the element embedding.

  • z_emb_dim (int) – Embedding dimension for atomic numbers.

  • n_heads (int) – Number of attention heads used for invariant scoring.

__init__(atom_dim, irreps_node, e_dim, hidden_dim, latent_dim, att_cutoff, attention_lmax, attention_irreps, rbf_dim=16, max_z=100, z_emb_dim=32, n_heads=4)[source]¶

Initialize AllAtomEquivariantAtomAttention.

forward(h, h_full, z, mask, e_feat, att_src, att_dst, att_dist, att_vec, absorber_mask=None)[source]¶

Compute equivariant energy-conditioned attention over atoms and return latent.

Parameters:

  • h (Tensor) – Invariant atom features (used for scoring), shape (B, N, H).

  • h_full (Tensor) – Full equivariant atom features (TP input for values), shape (B, N, irreps_node.dim).

  • z (Tensor) – Atomic numbers, shape (B, N).

  • mask (Tensor) – Valid-atom mask, shape (B, N).

  • e_feat (Tensor) – Energy RBF features, shape (nE, dE).

  • att_src (Tensor) – Flat source indices (queries) into B*N, shape (E_att,).

  • att_dst (Tensor) – Flat destination indices (keys/values) into B*N, shape (E_att,).

  • att_dist (Tensor) – Pair distances in A, shape (E_att,).

  • att_vec (Tensor) – Pair displacement vectors (src->dst) in A, shape (E_att, 3).

  • absorber_mask (Tensor | None) – Optional (B, N) bool mask. When given, queries are restricted to atoms with True; other rows are returned as zeros.

Return type:

Tensor

Returns:

Latent tensor of shape (B, N, nE, latent_dim).

xanesnet.models.e3ee_full.layers.branch_equivariant module¶

class xanesnet.models.e3ee_full.layers.branch_equivariant.AllAtomEquivariantHead(irreps_node, e_dim, hidden_dim, out_dim)[source]¶

Bases: Module

Late equivariant head applied to every atom.

Applies energy-conditioned irrep-wise modulation to each atom’s equivariant feature, converts to invariants, then projects to out_dim.

Parameters:

  • irreps_node (Irreps) – Irreps of the input equivariant features.

  • e_dim (int) – Dimension of the energy RBF embedding.

  • hidden_dim (int) – Hidden dimension of all internal MLPs.

  • out_dim (int) – Output (latent) dimension.

__init__(irreps_node, e_dim, hidden_dim, out_dim)[source]¶

Initialize AllAtomEquivariantHead.

forward(h_full, e_feat)[source]¶

Modulate, extract invariants, and project equivariant atom features.

Parameters:

  • h_full (Tensor) – Equivariant features for every atom, shape (B, N, D).

  • e_feat (Tensor) – Energy RBF features, shape (nE, e_dim).

Return type:

Tensor

Returns:

Latent tensor of shape (B, N, nE, out_dim).

class xanesnet.models.e3ee_full.layers.branch_equivariant.EnergyIrrepModulation(irreps, e_dim, hidden_dim)[source]¶

Bases: Module

Energy-conditioned scalar modulation of each irrep copy, preserving equivariance.

A scalar gate per irrep copy is predicted from the energy embedding and applied multiplicatively to all dim-dimensional components of that copy. Because a scalar multiplier commutes with rotations, equivariance is preserved.

Input x has shape (M, D); output broadcasts over energies to (M, nE, D).

Parameters:

  • irreps (Irreps) – The irreps specification of the equivariant features.

  • e_dim (int) – Dimension of the energy RBF embedding.

  • hidden_dim (int) – Hidden dimension of the internal MLP.

__init__(irreps, e_dim, hidden_dim)[source]¶

Initialize EnergyIrrepModulation.

forward(x, e_feat)[source]¶

Apply per-copy scalar energy modulation.

Parameters:

  • x (Tensor) – Equivariant features of shape (M, D) where M = B * N.

  • e_feat (Tensor) – Energy RBF features, shape (nE, e_dim).

Return type:

Tensor

Returns:

Modulated equivariant features of shape (M, nE, D).

xanesnet.models.e3ee_full.layers.branch_fusion module¶

Branch fusion layers for E3EEFull.

class xanesnet.models.e3ee_full.layers.branch_fusion.GatedBranchFusion(branch_dims, fused_dim, cond_dim, hidden_dim, use_softmax=True)[source]¶

Bases: Module

Energy-conditioned gated fusion of active branch latents.

Each active branch is projected to fused_dim and combined with a learned soft gate predicted from the projected branch values, their magnitudes, and a conditioning feature such as the energy embedding.

Parameters:

  • branch_dims (list[int]) – Feature dimensions of the active branch tensors.

  • fused_dim (int) – Common latent dimension used for gated summation.

  • cond_dim (int) – Dimension of the conditioning features.

  • hidden_dim (int) – Hidden dimension of the gate and output MLPs.

  • use_softmax (bool) – If True, normalize gates with softmax. Otherwise, use sigmoid gates normalized to sum to one.

__init__(branch_dims, fused_dim, cond_dim, hidden_dim, use_softmax=True)[source]¶

Initialize GatedBranchFusion.

reset_gate_logits()[source]¶

Initialize the final gate layer to produce uniform gates.

Return type:

None

forward(branches, cond_feat, return_gates=False)[source]¶

Fuse active branch tensors.

Parameters:

  • branches (list[Tensor]) – Active branch tensors with matching leading dimensions. Each tensor has shape (..., branch_dim_i).

  • cond_feat (Tensor) – Conditioning features of shape (..., cond_dim) or any trailing-leading shape broadcastable to the branches, such as (nE, cond_dim) for branch tensors shaped (B, N, nE, D).

  • return_gates (bool) – If True, return the normalized gate weights along with the fused tensor.

Return type:

Tensor | tuple[Tensor, Tensor]

Returns:

Fused tensor of shape (..., fused_dim). If return_gates is True, returns (fused, gates) where gates has shape (..., n_branches).

xanesnet.models.e3ee_full.layers.branch_path module¶

3-body path aggregation branch for E3EEFull.

class xanesnet.models.e3ee_full.layers.branch_path.PairElementEnergyScattering(max_z, z_emb_dim, e_dim, hidden_dim, out_dim)[source]¶

Bases: Module

Energy-conditioned element-pair scattering features (flat per-path).

Parameters:

  • max_z (int) – Maximum atomic number supported by the element embedding.

  • z_emb_dim (int) – Embedding dimension for atomic numbers.

  • e_dim (int) – Dimension of the energy RBF embedding.

  • hidden_dim (int) – Hidden dimension of the output MLP.

  • out_dim (int) – Output feature dimension per path.

__init__(max_z, z_emb_dim, e_dim, hidden_dim, out_dim)[source]¶

Initialize PairElementEnergyScattering.

forward(z_j, z_k, e_feat)[source]¶

Compute element-pair energy scattering features.

Parameters:

  • z_j (Tensor) – Atomic numbers of the j leg, shape (P,).

  • z_k (Tensor) – Atomic numbers of the k leg, shape (P,).

  • e_feat (Tensor) – Energy RBF features, shape (nE, e_dim).

Return type:

Tensor

Returns:

Scattering features of shape (P, nE, out_dim).

class xanesnet.models.e3ee_full.layers.branch_path.AllAtomPathAggregator(atom_dim, rbf_dim, geom_hidden_dim, scatter_dim, out_dim, cutoff)[source]¶

Bases: Module

Per-site 3-body path aggregator.

Consumes precomputed flat triplet scalars (r0j, r0k, rjk, cos(angle)) together with path_center – the flat atom index of the site (into the padded B * N layout) that each path belongs to. Paths are scatter-aggregated into the per-atom layout and projected.

Parameters:

  • atom_dim (int) – Dimension of invariant per-atom features.

  • rbf_dim (int) – Number of Gaussian RBF bases for distance encoding.

  • geom_hidden_dim (int) – Hidden dimension of the geometry MLP.

  • scatter_dim (int) – Intermediate scatter feature dimension.

  • out_dim (int) – Output (latent) dimension.

  • cutoff (float) – Radial cutoff in A used for the cosine envelope weights.

__init__(atom_dim, rbf_dim, geom_hidden_dim, scatter_dim, out_dim, cutoff)[source]¶

Initialize AllAtomPathAggregator.

forward(h_flat, z_flat, pair_elem_energy, e_feat, path_center, path_j, path_k, path_r0j, path_r0k, path_rjk, path_cosangle, bsz, n_atoms)[source]¶

Scatter 3-body path features into per-(atom, energy) latent vectors.

Parameters:

  • h_flat (Tensor) – Invariant atom features (flat), shape (B*N, atom_dim).

  • z_flat (Tensor) – Atomic numbers (flat), shape (B*N,).

  • pair_elem_energy (PairElementEnergyScattering) – Pre-constructed element-pair energy scattering module.

  • e_feat (Tensor) – Energy RBF features, shape (nE, e_dim).

  • path_center (Tensor) – Flat absorber-site index per path into B*N, shape (P,).

  • path_j (Tensor) – Flat atom index for leg j into B*N, shape (P,).

  • path_k (Tensor) – Flat atom index for leg k into B*N, shape (P,).

  • path_r0j (Tensor) – Absorber-to-j distance in A, shape (P,).

  • path_r0k (Tensor) – Absorber-to-k distance in A, shape (P,).

  • path_rjk (Tensor) – j-to-k distance in A, shape (P,).

  • path_cosangle (Tensor) – Cosine of the j-absorber-k angle, shape (P,).

  • bsz (int) – Batch size.

  • n_atoms (int) – Padded atoms per sample (N_max).

Return type:

Tensor

Returns:

Latent tensor of shape (B, N, nE, out_dim).

xanesnet.models.e3ee_full.layers.interactions module¶

Equivariant message-passing interaction block for E3EEFull.

class xanesnet.models.e3ee_full.layers.interactions.EquivariantInteractionBlock(irreps_node, irreps_sh, irreps_message, rbf_dim, radial_hidden_dim, cutoff, residual_scale_init=0.1)[source]¶

Bases: Module

Single equivariant message-passing interaction block using e3nn tensor products with gating.

Parameters:

  • irreps_node (str) – Irreps of the per-atom node features (input and output).

  • irreps_sh (str) – Irreps of the spherical harmonics on each edge.

  • irreps_message (str) – Intermediate irreps of the message before gating.

  • rbf_dim (int) – Number of Gaussian RBF features encoding edge lengths.

  • radial_hidden_dim (int) – Hidden dimension of the radial weight MLP.

  • cutoff (float) – Smooth cutoff radius in A; edges beyond this have zero weight.

  • residual_scale_init (float) – Initial value for the learnable residual scale.

__init__(irreps_node, irreps_sh, irreps_message, rbf_dim, radial_hidden_dim, cutoff, residual_scale_init=0.1)[source]¶

Initialize EquivariantInteractionBlock.

forward(x, edge_src, edge_dst, edge_sh, edge_rbf, edge_len)[source]¶

Apply one equivariant message-passing step.

Parameters:

  • x (Tensor) – Node features of shape (B*N, irreps_node.dim).

  • edge_src (Tensor) – Source flat indices into B*N, shape (E,).

  • edge_dst (Tensor) – Destination flat indices into B*N, shape (E,).

  • edge_sh (Tensor) – Spherical harmonics on each edge, shape (E, irreps_sh.dim).

  • edge_rbf (Tensor) – RBF-encoded edge lengths, shape (E, rbf_dim).

  • edge_len (Tensor) – Raw edge lengths in A, shape (E,).

Return type:

Tensor

Returns:

Updated node features of shape (B*N, irreps_node.dim).
